title: cron.rs SCHEDULES_FILE is a relative path producing cwd-dependent silent state
description:
## Severity
Major

## Dimension
Security and Access / Functional Correctness

## Location
src/tools/cron.rs:14 (const SCHEDULES_FILE) plus 35-55 (load/save)

## Violation
```rust
const SCHEDULES_FILE: &str = ".openclaudia/schedules.json";
```

ScheduleStore::load and save use the relative path verbatim:

```rust
fn load() -> Self {
    let path = PathBuf::from(SCHEDULES_FILE);
    if path.exists() { ... }
}
```

This couples the cron subsystem to whatever the process cwd happens to be at call time. Combined with worktree.rs mutating cwd (see separate issue), every cron operation can resolve to a DIFFERENT schedules.json depending on which worktree the agent is currently in. Worse, load() returns Default on any parse failure (line 39: `.unwrap_or_default()`) — corruption silently wipes the schedule list and save() then commits the empty store.

Test cron_create_then_list_round_trip changes cwd to a temp dir for this exact reason — the function is unsafe outside that pattern.

## Mandated refactor
1. Anchor schedules.json to PROJECT_ROOT (the canonical static in src/tools/file/mod.rs:78). Make load/save take a base PathBuf or read PROJECT_ROOT to construct the absolute path once.
2. On parse failure, do NOT return Default — return an error so the caller can refuse to clobber the existing (corrupt-but-recoverable) file. Move the corrupt file aside with a .corrupt timestamp suffix before recreating.
3. Consider injecting a ScheduleStore::Provider trait so tests do not need to mutate process cwd to redirect storage.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
